items.headline: John Sterling, theatrical Yankees broadcaster known for enduring home run calls, dies at 87
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Jon Sterling, the theatrical New York Yankees broadcaster known for extravagant, individualized home run calls, has died, the team and radio station WFAN announced Monday. He was 87.
Sterling had undergone heart bypass surgery this winter and after the procedure was attended by health care aides at his home in Edgewater, New Jersey.

items.headline: This Date in Baseball - Pete Rose became the youngest and 14th player with 3,000 hits
items.description:
May 5
1904 — Cy Young of the Red Sox pitched a perfect game against the Philadelphia Athletics, beating Rube Waddell 3-0. Having pitched nine hitless innings in two previous efforts, he ran his string of hitless innings to 18.

items.headline: Chase Elliott had never before won 2 Cup races this early in a NASCAR season
items.description:
FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — Chase Elliott never before had won two Cup races this early in a NASCAR season.
After first winning at Martinsville at the end of March, the most popular driver in the series opened May in Victory Lane at Texas. That 1 1/2-mile track is growing on Elliott after he had not been much of a fan of the repave and reconfiguration done there nine years ago.

items.headline: Jamahl Mosley fired as coach of Orlando Magic, AP source says
items.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Jamahl Mosley fired as coach of Orlando Magic, AP source says.

items.headline: Magic fire Jamahl Mosley after 5 seasons as coach, 3 first-round playoff appearances
items.description:
Jamahl Mosley was fired as coach of the Orlando Magic on Monday, paying the widely expected price after the team blew a 3-1 series lead and got eliminated by the Detroit Pistons in Round 1 of the Eastern Conference playoffs.
It was Orlando’s third consecutive first-round playoff exit, and easily the most disappointing. Not only did the eighth-seeded Magic lose all three chances to upset the top-seeded Pistons, but one of those games saw Orlando have a 24-point second-half lead at home and still lose. Orlando missed 23 consecutive shots in that Game 6 loss on Friday, getting booed by fans when it was over.

items.headline: Nearly half of Munetaka Murakami's hits have been homers. It takes a lot to sustain that
items.description:
After three straight 100-loss seasons — including a record 121 defeats in 2024 — the Chicago White Sox needed a little excitement.
Munetaka Murakami has certainly provided that.

items.headline: Coach Marcelino to leave Villarreal at end of season
items.description: Villarreal coach Marcelino will leave at the end of the season despite guiding the Yellow Submarines into next season's Champions League...

items.headline: Wrexham's Premier League dream is on hold but its global fanbase can keep dreaming
items.description:
WREXHAM, Wales (AP) — There will be no trips to Old Trafford or Anfield. No visits from Erling Haaland or Bukayo Saka.
For Wrexham and its growing fanbase across the world, the dream of playing in the Premier League next season — of back-to-back-to-back-to back promotions — is over.
